content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Thurman White 
Friends of Gwen Moore 
c/o 34294 Dunhill Dr. 
Fremont, CA 94555 
Dear Mr. White: 
September 21, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-517 
We have received your letter (copy enclosed) seeking written 
confirmation of the telephone advice provided to you on August 29, 
1989 .. This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects 
current law and the advice I provided to you. 
In Taxpayers To Limit Campaign Spending v. Fair Political 
Practices commission (1989) 89 Daily Journal D.A.R. 9863, the 
Court of Appeal upheld the provisions of Proposition 68 banning 
off-year campaign fundraising. 
These provisions apply only to 
legislators and legislative candidates. 
Based on the language of the opinion, the ban on off-year 
fundraising is not effective until the ruling becomes final as to 
both the Court of Appeal and the State Supreme Court. 
The Commis-
sion has petitioned the Supreme Court to review the decision. 
We 
anticipate that the Supreme Court will not act on our petition 
before October 2, 1989, although we cannot predict when the Court 
will act. 
It is therefore permissible for legislative candidates 
to continue their fundraising activities until such time as the 
decision becomes final as a result of the actions of tbe Supreme 
Court. 
Consequently, assuming that the Supreme Court does not act to 
deny the Commission's petition for review prior to September 20, 
1989, the recognition event and fundraiser for Assemblywoman Gwen 
Moore to be held on September 20, 1989, would not be precluded by 
the Taxpayers decision. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322-5660 


File No. A-89-517 
Page 2 
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, 
do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:BMB:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
~~.~~tt-
By: 
Blanca M. Breeze 
Counsel, Legal Division 


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq. 
General Counsel 
FPPC 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
FPPC 
SEP I 8 '*2 AN '69 
AUGUST 30, 1989 
REQUEST FOR CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE RE IMPLICATIONS OF PROP. 68 
COURT OF APPEAL DECISION (No Off-Year Campaign Fundraising) FOR SEPTEMBER 20, 
1989 FUNDRAISER 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
This is to request written confirmation of telephone advice I received from 
Blanca Breeze on 8/29/89 as to whether the August 2 California Court of Appeal 
decision re Proposition 68 -- specifically the provision banning off-year campaign 
fundraising -- would preclude an upcoming recognition event and fundraiser for 
Assemblywoman Gwen Moore on September 20, 1989. 
Ms. Breeze advised that the earliest the Court's decision would become final 
would be October 2, 1989 and, therefore, the Court's decision would not preclude 
the planned event. 
Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please contact me if you have 
additional questions. 
Thurman White 
Friends of Gwen Moore 
c/o 34294 Dunhill Dr. 
Fremont, CA 
94555 


Kathryn E. Donovan, Esq. 
General Counsel 
FPPC 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
AUGUST 30. 1989 
REQUEST FOR CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE RE IMPLICATIONS OF PROP. 68 
COURT OF APPEAL DECISION (No Off-Year Campaign Fundraising) FOR SEPTEMBER 20, 
1989 FUNDRAISER 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
This is to request written confirmation of telephone advice I received from 
Blanca Breeze on 8/29/89 as to whether the August 2 California Court of Appeal 
decision re Proposition 68 -- specifically the provision banning off-year campaign 
fundraising -- would preclude an upcoming recognition event and fundraiser for 
Assemblywoman Gwen Moore on September 20. 1989. 
Ms. Breeze advised that the earliest the Court's decision would become final 
would be October 2, 1989 and, therefore, the Court's decision would not preclude 
the planned event. 
Thank you for your prompt attention to this request. Please contact me if you have 
additional questions. 
Thurman White 
Friends of Gwen Moore 
c/o 34294 Dunhill Dr. 
Fremont, CA 
94555 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Thurman White 
Friends of Gwen Moore 
34294 Dunhill Drive 
Fremont, CA 
94555 
Dear Mr. White: 
September 7, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-517 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on September 1, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Blanca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public ~'records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
K~ 
£, '7Jv-,~~ 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804 .. 0807 • (916)322 .. 5660

sections.question: Whether the August 2 California Court of Appeal decision re Proposition 68 -- specifically the provision banning off-year campaign fundraising -- would preclude an upcoming recognition event and fundraiser for Assemblywoman Gwen Moore on September 20, 1989.
sections.conclusion: It is therefore permissible for legislative candidates to continue their fundraising activities until such time as the decision becomes final as a result of the actions of the Supreme Court. Consequently, assuming that the Supreme Court does not act to deny the Commission's petition for review prior to September 20, 1989, the recognition event and fundraiser for Assemblywoman Gwen Moore to be held on September 20, 1989, would not be precluded by the Taxpayers decision.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the Court of Appeal's decision upholding Proposition 68's ban on off-year campaign fundraising preclude a legislative candidate fundraiser scheduled for September 20, 1989?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that the off-year fundraising ban is not effective until the Court of Appeal decision becomes final through Supreme Court action. Since the Supreme Court is unlikely to act before October 2, 1989, the planned September 20, 1989 fundraiser is permissible.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter responding to a verbal advice request. Contains both the incoming request letter(s) and the FPPC's formal written response. The response letter (dated September 21, 1989) contains the CONCLUSION section clearly stating the FPPC's advice. A preliminary acknowledgment letter dated September 7, 1989 is also included.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the August 2 California Court of Appeal decision re Proposition 68 -- specifically the provision banning off-year campaign fundraising -- would preclude an upcoming recognition event and fundraiser for Assemblywoman Gwen Moore on September 20, 1989.

CONCLUSION: It is therefore permissible for legislative candidates to continue their fundraising activities until such time as the decision becomes final as a result of the actions of the Supreme Court. Consequently, assuming that the Supreme Court does not act to deny the Commission's petition for review prior to September 20, 1989, the recognition event and fundraiser for Assemblywoman Gwen Moore to be held on September 20, 1989, would not be precluded by the Taxpayers decision.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding whether a Proposition 68 Court of Appeal decision banning off-year campaign fundraising would preclude a September 20, 1989 fundraiser for a legislative candidate. The advice confirms that since the decision will not be final until Supreme Court action (expected after October 2, 1989), the planned event may proceed.
